review_title: Left Dropbox (after 10 years) for Lucid. Glad I did!
description: Excellent. Highly recommend. A+++++++++
author: Tobin S.
pros: Integration into file structure. Much easier than Dropbox.
cons: No dislikes. Would not change anything.

review_title: Great, but it could use some quality-of-life improvements
description: he software works as intended — fast, simple, and stable. Apart from the SSO and Microsoft Store issues, I can’t help but wonder where the catch is. So far, we’re very satisfied with it.
author: Robert R.
pros: So far, it just works — easy to install, stable data streaming. A modern cloud solution with unmatched ease of use.
cons: The fact that only one domain can be configured for SSO makes this feature unusable for us, as we manage four different companies. The SSO integration itself is also inconvenient, as a client-side SSO key file has to be imported for it to work. SSO is supposed to be a secure and simple login method, not one that requires distributing the same key to all users so they can manually import it when needed. Additionally, it would be very practical if the app were available in the Microsoft App Store, as this would eliminate the need to manually create a new package for every update in a managed environment.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I’m very satisfied with my overall experience with lucid. It actually helps our project management easier.
author: Kathleen F.
pros: Ease of use. It’s easy to understand and navigate. No one needs a tutorial when cause it’s a very straightforward application.
cons: When I had problems and contacted support, there were a lot of technical terms that I had a hard time understanding. Good thing the agent that I was talking to had a lot of patience.

description: Overall going from classic to new lucid link has been a breeze and I believe with a few minor changes the service can be phenomenal.
author: Noah C.
pros: I love Lucid Links ability to give my team and I access to our media files. It has created a incredible system for me to manage and share files with our freelancers.
cons: The new lucid link UI is something I think needs improvement. The overall ui from classic allowed me to have better control/understanding of the overall software. Additionally I find it much harder to migrate my cache due to it being limited to a command function

description: Great, no complaints at all. Easy to use. Small application that takes seconds to install. Super fast feedback from the support team
author: Julius F.
pros: Ease of use, great customer support when having issues, with almost instant response from the technical team.
cons: There is really nothing about lucidlink that I do not like, its always easy to use with minimal issues.

description: Excelent, I'd work with LucidLink 100% any time I can. I've never work with a cloud-like app so fast and so efficiently.
author: Sebastian L.
pros: The fast environment to work on, the great UX/UI of the app overall, the good system of recovery when accidental deletes.
cons: Had some trouble with special characters like "´" or "ñ" and the fact that if it goes down (like when AWS went down) you are a bit dependent.

author: Jamie F.
pros: Opens on startup and no waiting times. I’m able to keep my work on a drive that’s reliable and always accessible. Easy to use too and the customer support is very helpful
cons: The only bad experience I’ve had with LucidLink was that there was an error and the app didn’t want to open, but I believe that was a cache issue
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: As a video editor. LucidLink has fairly seamlessly been able to move me from using hardware based server and the stacks of hard drives.
author: James G.
pros: Ease of use, speed and flexibility. Full HD video streaming/editing with the proper internet connection.
cons: Setup and cache size needed at times. Can be tricky to get third party vendors to setup. Need a large high speed drive for cache drive.

review_title: Good but glitchy and not totally polished.
description: Good but glitchy and not totally polished.
author: Michael W.
pros: Works well enough, better than Dropbox overall for video editing but has its issues as well.
cons: When I hit 1 TB of usage my service was completely cut off without any warning or explanation. It totally wrecked an end of day deadline I had.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Would be hands down the champion, IF they showed respect for MSPs who could resell to endless clients
description: decent, we are manually moving data ourselves as opposed to a canned solution, but again no white labeling and no discounts for MSP. Unless you can do unrealistic numbers of TB.
author: Luke G.
pros: Speed and ease of use. The app is convenient but not unique.
cons: As an MSP who could sell this to many clients we are very picky about who we convert from other solutions due to no MSP partner program at all. No whitelisting, no actual incentive to sell it to our clients.

description: I am enjoying it very much. It makes my live easier.
author: Moritz B.
pros: Its a very easy Workflow. For me as a remote employee its an absolute blessing.
cons: The installation on my Mac is a bit complicated. But support is very good.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: feature film vfx company successful user
description: Love, love, love it. Our whole company moved over to it and now everything just works. Can't sing it praises high enough.
author: Matthew T.
pros: We call it dropbox on steriods. So fast and hardly any issues ever. seemless.
cons: Occasional needs to recalculate itself. cost is creeping up to high figures now we're using more and more data.

description: Practically seamless use - once set up largely it is "set and forget"
author: Bruce M.
pros: The speed of transfer of files for Final Cut Pro libraries with Pin feature
cons: randomly slow internet speeds tht affect transfer rates

description: I have been looking for a solution for migrating local data to the cloud for a long time. Normal s3 object storage does not offer such security in the form of file encryption and such quick access to files. Lucidlink enabled me to migrate business files to the cloud.
author: Magdalena S.
pros: LucidLink enables the use of s3 object storage as a cloud file storage. Simple to install. Very simple in administering users and access shares. I use LucidLink with my own s3 object storage as the central company file server. Thanks to this solution, authorized employees have access to the necessary files. I also use LucidLink as a backup target. The perfect solution,
cons: For starters, it's difficult to understand how to make the most of LucidLink. It seems that you can use many different file spaces simultaneously, but in practice it has not worked. It's best to use one file space on one device.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: LucidLink has transformed the way we work and streamlined our move to the cloud.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Speed of File Access has transformed the way we work particularly for our M&E team.
cons: Permission structure is quite basic and restrictive.

description: First time using cloud streaming storage and could not be more impressed
author: Tim S.
pros: powerful and easy to use, especially the proxy pinning
cons: gotta have a lot of Solid State Drive space, but easy work around with bootable external
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Vitor D.
pros: What I like most about LucidLink is the seamless ability to manage projects and work with diverse collaborators remotely. The platform's interface stands out for its simplicity and user-friendliness, making it incredibly easy to onboard new users. This ease of use ensures that team members can quickly adapt to the system and focus on their tasks without being bogged down by a steep learning curve. Additionally, the real-time collaboration features allow for efficient communication and workflow, enhancing productivity and making it feel as though we are working in the same physical space, even when we are miles apart.
cons: One of the main drawbacks I've encountered with LucidLink is the issue with updating the application on the day new updates are released. This has caused some disruptions to my workflow. Additionally, I frequently face network problems that require more technical knowledge than an average end-user might possess. These issues include PIN problems and intermittent connection losses. Despite these challenges, I must commend the support team, as they are always quick and efficient in resolving any issues I encounter.

review_title: This is the future of remote video editing
description: Excellent. For the couple of issues we had, their tech support was extremely prompt and helpful.
author: Brian B.
pros: Simple, inexpensive and reliable. It really feels like a local drive.
cons: I have no complaints. There were a couple of times where performance lagged, but it turned out to be a local problem.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Has been great so far. The people working at LucidLink are responsive and care about our success.
author: Brian K.
pros: Very fast response time for troubleshooting IT issues
cons: Some lag during playback of video. This is sporadic

author: STUART W.
pros: We needed a platform to share footage in a tight timeline and Lucid was the answer
cons: So far - nothing to not like . The customer service has been outstanding and that is a huge key to any successful company
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: FANTASTIC overall experience. Their support is top notch on the rare occassions we have had to use support,
author: Orin L.
pros: The remote editing workflow simply works. It's been the Holy Grail of editing for a decade to be able to truly edit from content in the cloud. There have been many failures by other companies and LucidLink finally cracked the code even with very low bandwidth for very remote users.
cons: There isn't much not to like here. If there was one thing, and this isn't even on LucidLink themselves, it is finding a suitable cloud provider to host content. Lucid offers a few and you have to REALLY ask some tough questions to determine if the Tier 1 or Tier 2 storage is right for your needs.

author: Alessandro M.
pros: The possibility to have a could storage optimized for video projects is exactly what I was looking for my team. Living and working in this time where the team can be dislocated around the world everybody has the possibility to work in a collaborative way on the same projects without the need to waiting hours to download files and always to remember to upload an updated version
cons: To have a good experience with LucidLink is mandatory to have a strong Internet connection, even if the "chace system" help you to to work also with an ADSL

description: LucidLink eliminates the need for our data handlers to deal with S3 or SFTP connections. With LucidLink all of the data appears on their workstation just like files on their native file system. We regularly find new ways to leverage LucidLink to simplify our workflows.
author: Mike M.
pros: Our private data is not available on the local file system unless the computer is on and logged in to LucidLink. This important layer of security combined with the ease of use of a locally mounted volume makes LucidLink a killer application for accessible and secure shared files.
cons: I wish LucidLink could be layered on top of existing S3 buckets full of files. Sadly, that's not possible.

description: Unparalleled customer service; incredible response time to chat questions and professional replies.
author: Irvin L.
pros: Ease of use in a complex environment, easy to understand and plenty of support.
cons: limited low price offer; I hope they can keep it at its current rate.

review_title: Has made it easier to sync files and share them with customers.
description: Our efforts to improve project collaboration have shown impressive results. Fixing synchronization issues and searching for data across several servers used to take up a lot of our work. Lucidlink has made everything organized and accessible from any location. As part of our process for generating large-scale projects for important clients, we utilized it to share massive design files with our marketing team and external partners. With real-time file access and updates, the review and approval process became more easier.
author: Marek R.
pros: It took too long to access and share massive files; there were regular issues with downloads and synchronizations; our design files are typically big and spread out across multiple servers; and there were continual problems with file synchronization and sharing with external clients and collaborators. With LucidLink, we can keep working as a local unit while still using our regular apps and processes. The opportunity to collaborate in real-time has increased both our productivity and creativity. It has been a lifesaver for our team, particularly when dealing with large design files.
cons: Many clients were still concerned about the security of their cloud-based projects, even though LucidLink had strong security measures in place, such as zero-knowledge encryption. To ensure the safety of their data, we had to work closely with them to explain the security procedures that were put in place.
